# Evidence Strength Scoring Rubric

Use this rubric to avoid overstating weak evidence.

| Score | Evidence type | How to use it |
|---:|---|---|
| 5 | Regulator/court/platform enforcement record | Main claim support |
| 4 | Official platform policy or platform integrity report | Boundary-setting support |
| 4 | Peer-reviewed academic source | Conceptual/theoretical support |
| 3 | Reputable journalism with documents or named sources | Case-study support |
| 2 | Industry artifact or vendor material | Market-structure evidence |
| 1 | Reddit/community/operator chatter | Sentiment or anecdotal context only |
| 0 | Anonymous unsourced claim | Do not use except as a question to investigate |

## Safe wording by score

- Score 5: “documented,” “reported by regulator,” “enforced against.”
- Score 4: “policy prohibits,” “research suggests,” “platform states.”
- Score 3: “reported,” “according to investigation.”
- Score 2: “industry materials describe,” “vendor markets the practice as.”
- Score 1: “anecdotal discussion,” “operator chatter,” “not independently verified.”
- Score 0: “unverified claim.”

## Rule

Do not build the main thesis on score-1 material. Use low-score material only to show language, incentives, or questions for further research.
